title: Topology Declarations, Property Equivalence, Policies, and Ownership · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent integration team receives different traffic, tenant, topology, schema, and failure constraints and transfers topology declarations, property equivalence, policies, and ownership into a new versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned topology declaration and ownership manifest with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams.
Make repeated declarations equivalent in type, durability, exclusivity, auto-delete, and immutable arguments and diagnose precondition mismatch.
Choose durable, exclusive server-named, auto-delete, and queue-TTL lifecycles from responsibility and race evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Topology Owner Contract
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab messaging team handles case 8093 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Topology Owner Contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for assign ownership for exchanges, queues, bindings, policies, migrations, rollback, and deletion across application and platform teams. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Topology Owner Contract, keep Topology Owner Contract connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A lab messaging team handles case 4141 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to protect property equivalence and route mutable operations through policy or versioned migration; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
